L1.area: HTTP server request handling and file serving logic

L2.description: A path traversal vulnerability exists where the application fails to restrict file access to the intended directory when serving files via an HTTP route. An attacker can craft a URL with path traversal sequences to read arbitrary files on the system. Authentication is disabled by default, widening the attack surface to unauthenticated users.

L3.area: Log file serving and download route handling in the web server layer
L3.description: A path traversal vulnerability exists where the application constructs file paths from user-supplied URL segments when serving log files via an HTTP route, without verifying the resolved path stays within the configured log directory. An attacker can use path traversal sequences to escape the intended directory and read arbitrary files on the system. Authentication is disabled by default, allowing unauthenticated exploitation.

ground_truth.reason: The /log/ HTTP route handler constructs file paths from user-supplied URL segments without verifying the resolved path stays within the intended log directory, allowing path traversal to read arbitrary files on the system.
